mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically elevating equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals accelerating labor market recovery, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of instances where the threshold is crossed.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand across durable and real estate sectors, generating robust corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth during a supply-constrained expansion triggers inflation expectations and margin compression fears, causing equity markets to price in tighter policy and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in more than 65% of instances when industrial production rises rapidly in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation combined with persistently low nominal policy rates compresses real yields, triggering expectations of abrupt monetary tightening that suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict direction with greater than 55% accuracy over a 24-month walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between payroll growth and forward returns remains below 0.05 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent confirms robust macroeconomic expansion, leading to upward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters where the GDP growth threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indices signal strengthening manufacturing output and business investment, which translates into higher corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations or loses predictive power in out-of-sample validation.
